feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /mu/ Music: There is no hard rock or heavy metal band that rips it harder than April Wine. N…
entry.content: There is no hard rock or heavy metal band that rips it harder than April Wine. Name any random metal band, AC/DC, Judas Priest, Van Halen, they mostly have slow songs. April Wine has mostly all high energy rippers that you just jam out to. Miles the lead singer left the band just as they finally made it world wide out of burn out from so much touring. They had so much potential if they would have kept going. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=usW QOPTORn8 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Ylc Y_enzwm

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Is plastic surgery getting out of control in Hollywood or is it simply more visi…
entry.content: Is plastic surgery getting out of control in Hollywood or is it simply more visible these days? Even male actors are getting extreme face lips and fillers. What's causing this phenomenon? Cheaper access to surgeons? I thought the woke movement was supposed to prevent this stuff but it feels like it's worse than ever before.

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Brian Duffield Rumored To Be Writing New Alien Film
entry.content: >According to a report from writer Daniel Richtman, it sounds as if Brian Duffield (No One Will Save You, The Babysitter, Spontaneous), is writing a new Alien film for 20th Century Studios. Richtman notes that at this time, it’s unclear if Duffield will direct the film, as well. The news come on the heels of Duffield’s adaptation of Daniel Kraus’ novel, Whalefall, set to hit theaters this October. >If true, Duffield is someone who may not have been the first to come to mind for a new Alien franc

entry.title: /int/ International: /int/ sold me a lie. They said poland was an economic powerhouse, joining the 1 …
entry.content: /int/ sold me a lie. They said poland was an economic powerhouse, joining the 1 trillion club. But in reality, the polish economy isn't any miracle happening. Inflation is the vital reason why poland appears to grow so fast, but their real GDP adjusted for inflation shows their economy still is at 500 billion dollars. Meaning their actual economy is over 50% smaller than what /int/ says. Only a brainlet polish nationalist living in dusseldorf would believe Poland doubled their economy since 2020

entry.title: /o/ Auto: I bought my car new, it's 4 years old with less than 30k miles. But it has gotte…
entry.content: I bought my car new, it's 4 years old with less than 30k miles. But it has gotten dinged by a rock and has a scratch on the bumper somehow. How the fuck do you guys stop this? All the stuff I planned to do with it went out the window because why put money into a broken car? I don't understand how people keep their cars in perfect condition.

entry.title: /trash/ Off-Topic: /flg/ - F-List General #8389 Buck Broken Edition Previous Thread: >>83553430 F-l…
entry.content: /flg/ - F-List General #8389 Buck Broken Edition Previous Thread: >>83553430 F-list is a website for people interested in erotic roleplaying, and creating character profiles to find like-minded partners with similar interests and fetishes to roleplay with. It features a webchat (which can be used for textual roleplaying), group forums and more kinks and depravity than you can memorize. The purpose of this thread is to serve as a place where roleplayers can freely share and advertise their profil

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Experiences with AI vibecoding
entry.content: So I'm just about done with my first client/server app written entirely with the help of Codex. Angular frontend, C#/.NET backend REST API, it's a typical SaaS style app. I've worked at 3 different SaaS companies prior to this (10+ year senior engineer) so I have a good amount of reference material regarding the quality of this vs what I've worked on/used. It feels like my role has transitioned to some kind of hybrid architect/PO/QA position, with only light engineering interruptions. It's hones

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Linux Gaming Thread
entry.content: I downloaded Baldur's Gate I Enhanced Edition which actually had a Linux port on GOG. I tried running it but it was missing libssl 1.0, of which Arch Linux now uses a newer version. Thankfully, an openssl 1.0 package was preserved in the Arch archive so that I can download and install it manually. My question is, why is this such an issue in Linux but not in Windows? Why can't an older program just use the installed newer libs? My fixing today was quite advanced for average noobs. How does Windo

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Lazenby was not a great Bond, but Telly Savalas was unquestionably the best Blof…
entry.content: Lazenby was not a great Bond, but Telly Savalas was unquestionably the best Blofeld. Donald Pleasance was trash, Christoph Waltz was trash. Telly reigned supreme, and we should all take a moment to appreciate that.
